training_types_mentioned: CIMT (Constraint Induced Movement Therapy) | Daily Massage and Stretching | Ankle Weights | EMS (Electrical Muscle Stimulation) | TENS (Transcutaneous Electrical Nerve Stimulation) | Water Training / Pool Activities | Sports (Football, Golf, Hockey, Skating) | DMI
outcome_domains_mentioned: Motor development | Gait stability | Hand coordination and asymmetry
perceived_effect_summary: Continuous progress over time. Achieved milestones in correct age range.
development_over_time: The text describes development across multiple age periods (1, 2, and 3 years old). Milestones achieved on schedule (Crawling at 11 months, Walking at 14 months). Some regression or increased asymmetry during growth spurts was noted but stabilized later.
barriers: Limited access to intensive training in Sweden | Difficulty accessing DMI therapy | Minimal support from habilitation services
facilitators: Parent-led rehabilitation | International parent forums and networks | Self-directed research and information gathering
supporting_quotes: We had to shoulder the role of the child's primary physical therapists. | Our frustration is about the limited access to intensive training and specialized rehabilitation in Sweden.
